scenarios.title: An abandoned finding survives a round trip through the store
scenarios.description: Finding F-093 — the stage-adjustment collapse — is the demo's payload. It must be writable with its reason intact and readable back with the reason attached, because the reason IS the value.
preconditions: The graph store is migrated to the Breadcrumbs-v2 vocabulary
steps: Write F-093 with status 'abandoned' and a reason explaining the effect collapsed after stage adjustment
expected: The finding is returned with status 'abandoned' and its reason text intact
The reason is not truncated or dropped

scenarios.title: An abandoned finding without a reason is rejected
scenarios.description: The human gate. An abandoned attempt with no stated reason is worthless to the next researcher, so the store refuses it rather than accepting a hollow row.
steps: Attempt to write a finding with status 'abandoned' and no reason field
expected: The write is rejected with an error naming the finding

scenarios.title: Migrating the committed database preserves every row and edge
scenarios.description: ingestion/cairn.db is committed and shared by the team. The CHECK-constraint rebuild drops and recreates the findings table, and finding_edges cascades on delete — so a careless migration silently destroys the graph. Run it twice; nothing may change the second time.
preconditions: A copy of the committed ingestion/cairn.db with at least one session, finding, and edge
steps: Record row counts for chat_messages, findings, and finding_edges
Run the migration a second time
Re-read the row counts and the live DDL from sqlite_master
expected: Row counts are unchanged after both runs — no edge was cascade-deleted
The findings CHECK constraint now admits 'open' and the edge CHECK admits 'duplicate_of'
The second run is a no-op
